other_observers: Christian Nunes, Bill Schmoker, Cole Wild, Maggie Boswell, and David Waltman.; Christian Nunes found the bird at 3pm the same day.
duration: Brief observations over one hour period
specific_location: Davidson Mesa which is just east of Marshall and a few miles south of Boulder
description: This bird was the same size as a Vesper Sparrow that was seen while chasing it. It was overall a brownish bird above and had at least two white outer tail feathers on each side. It had a large dark eye which contrasted with the lighter brown face, and the crown was darker. When seen on the ground it had two thin white wing bars and patterned wing coverts and upper back. The bill was long and the legs were pinkish. The throat was white but I never saw the front of the bird well.Behaviors: The bird flushed several times while we chased it. It had a characteristic flight and went to the ground swiftly every time it landed. It then ran through the short vegetation and was very difficult to see well.Call: None heardPlumage: Basic
